title: Aggressive Cows
description: You have n stalls at positions x_1, ..., x_n along a line. You want to place k cows in stalls such that the minimum distance between any two cows is maximized. Find this maximum possible minimum distance.
input_spec: The first line contains two integers n and k (2 <= k <= n <= 10^5). The second line contains n distinct integers x_i (0 <= x_i <= 10^9).

routes.name: Binary search on the answer
routes.description: Binary search on the minimum distance d. For each candidate d, greedily check if k cows can be placed with pairwise distance >= d.

observations.title: Monotonic feasibility
observations.description: If you can place k cows with min distance d, you can also place them with any distance < d. This monotonicity enables binary search.

hints.nudge: Is there a monotonic relationship between the distance and feasibility?
hints.question: If placing cows with min gap d works, does min gap d-1 also work? What does this monotonicity suggest?
hints.formal: Feasibility is monotonically decreasing: feasible(d) implies feasible(d') for all d' < d. Binary search on d to find the maximum feasible value.

observations.title: Greedy check function
observations.description: To check if distance d is feasible: sort stalls, place first cow at stalls[0], then greedily place each next cow at the first stall >= last + d.

hints.nudge: Given a candidate distance d, how would you check if it's achievable?
hints.question: If stalls are sorted and you want gaps >= d, where should you place the first cow? The second?
hints.formal: Sort stalls. Place cow at stalls[0]. For each next cow, find first stall >= last_position + d. If you place >= k cows, d is feasible.

observations.title: Binary search bounds
observations.description: lo = 1 (minimum possible distance), hi = (max_position - min_position) / (k-1) or simply max_position - min_position.

hints.nudge: What's the smallest and largest possible answer?
hints.question: The minimum distance is at least 1. What's the theoretical maximum? How do you set hi?
hints.formal: lo = 1, hi = (stalls[n-1] - stalls[0]). Binary search while lo <= hi: mid = (lo+hi)/2, if feasible(mid) then lo = mid+1 else hi = mid-1. Answer = hi.

observations.title: Sort stalls first
observations.description: The greedy check requires stalls to be sorted. Sort them once before binary search begins.

hints.nudge: Does the order of stalls matter for the greedy check?
hints.question: Can you greedily place cows if stalls are in random order? What preprocessing helps?
hints.formal: Sort the stall positions O(n log n) once. Then each feasibility check is O(n) and binary search does O(log(max_dist)) checks.
